findings.problem: The chapter lands Daro's grievance about being used as an instrument at the end of the rescue scene, then replays essentially the same accusation at the landing without enough new leverage. Because the later argument restates the thesis rather than forcing a fresh turn, the final quarter spends tension explaining an already-legible power struggle instead of sharpening it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework either the exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:201 or the landing fight beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:365 so the chapter carries one clear 'Riva uses people' beat, not two parallel versions. In the later scene, make the conflict turn on a new local pressure point already on the page: Nami's worsening hand at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:349, the sixth-mouth vision raised at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:409, or Riva's abandonment guilt surfaced at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:381.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:201 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:365, a reviewer should be able to name one distinct emotional turn in each scene; if both scenes still reduce to 'Daro says Riva treats family as tools,' the revision fails.

findings.problem: Daro's later accusations drift out of his embodied, half-devotional register and into clean abstract indictment. Phrases such as 'weight you throw on every scale,' 'Everything in your mouth turns into cargo,' and 'duty purified' read more like generic literary family-argument rhetoric than this specific character's throat/song/current-driven voice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Daro's accusatory lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:209, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:387, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:419 so the pressure comes through his own idiom: bodily cost, song, current, omen, hunger, or shame. Use the local benchmark of his tighter, more character-specific lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:147 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:153 as calibration, without repeating those lines.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchange spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:365 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:420, at least one of Daro's objections must be unmistakably his because it fuses accusation with bodily or devotional imagery; if the lines could be reassigned to Lale or to a generic sibling quarrel without sounding wrong, the revision fails.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly sharpens meaning through the same negation-pivot sentence shape: 'not X / only Y,' 'he did not ... he did not ...,' or 'no one told ... no one told ...'. Used this often, the maneuver stops feeling pointed and starts feeling like a prose tic, flattening rhythm across otherwise strong scenes.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:299, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:361, replace several of the negation pivots with direct image, action, or plain assertion so the distinctions land through fresher syntax rather than repeated correction.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised chapter, no more than two of the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:299, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:361 may still rely on the same negation-to-assertion pattern; the rest must make their turn through materially different sentence shapes or concrete image.

summary: Booker passes: the chapter lands the required ordeal beats, keeps close-third past-tense control, escalates Nami's wound into a consequential fever, and gives the sixth-mouth mystery a concrete and continuity-consistent shape. Craft, dialogue, and prose fail because the Riva-Daro power struggle is stated twice without enough new leverage, Daro's later lines slide toward abstract interchangeable rhetoric, and the prose develops a noticeable negation-pivot tic.
